Serena Analysis: RuntimeStore Mutation Authority (P15)
=======================================================

Query: callers of RuntimeStore::acquireWriteAccess
Result: RuntimePublicationCoordinator::create() — single entry point (core/RuntimePublicationCoordinator.h:96)

Query: callers of WriteAccess -publishAndSwap
Result: RuntimePublicationCoordinator::publishWorld() — single mutation path
  - publishAndSwap() is the ONLY mutation method on WriteAccess
  - publishAndSwap is called exclusively from RuntimePublicationCoordinator::publishWorld()

Query: callers of RuntimePublicationCoordinator::publishWorld
Result: AudioEngine::publishWorld() — AudioEngine.h:2754
  - coordinator.publishWorld(std::move(worldOwner)) — delegates to template coordinator

Query: Direct callers of coordinator.publishWorld in AudioEngine
Result: 11 call sites across:
  - AudioEngine.Init.cpp:54
  - AudioEngine.Processing.ReleaseResources.cpp:134
  - AudioEngine.Commit.cpp:911, 948, 1011, 1052, 1221, 1400
  - AudioEngine.Processing.PrepareToPlay.cpp:132, 244
  - AudioEngine.Timer.cpp:405

Conclusion: WriteAccess capability is exclusively issued by
RuntimePublicationCoordinator::create(). No other code path can obtain WriteAccess.
ISR coordinator (convo::isr::RuntimePublicationCoordinator) provides
commit()/retire() as the only mutation interfaces, also exclusively from coordinator.
